Here is a polite and structured English review you can use:


---

Review:

One of the main issues I noticed in After the Fall is the lack of a proper flashlight system and the limited immersion when interacting with collectible items such as disks (floppies/data items).

In dark environments, the absence of a functional flashlight or better visibility tools can make gameplay unnecessarily difficult, especially in certain map areas where lighting is poor. This reduces comfort and can affect the overall experience of exploration and combat.

Additionally, the way collectible items are handled feels less immersive. Picking up disks does not fully integrate them into a clear inventory system, which can create confusion about whether the item was actually collected or not. A more interactive or visible inventory system would significantly improve immersion and player satisfaction.

These issues are especially important for accessibility. Improving visibility options and adding better interaction feedback would make the game more enjoyable for players with weaker eyesight or visual sensitivity.

I hope the developers consider improving these aspects in future updates, as the core gameplay is strong and very enjoyable.

Ignore all the people who are complaining about the graphics. Its not like 8k HD, that's for sure, but they look almost exactly like Half Life 2 or Titanfall graphics, and i think that's what they were going for. I like it, it adds charm to the game. 

The gun handling is somewhat janky that's for sure, but it can be REALLY satisfying when you get a perfect reload. (Which isn't hard to do) the shooting feels good, the recoil is manageable and pretty realistic, the guns are accurate and not hard to aim, and the gun models themselves are very well done and detailed. 

The game CAN get repetitive, sure. It could definitely use a real single player campaign. It already has one, but its not very immersive, and pretty short. The game needs a single player campaign where you're lost and have to find your way back to the hub or something like that, not with levels that let you return to the hub after every completed mission. Its still fun if you have a few friends to play it with, because there's always some kind of chaos, but a good campaign would be great.

I've seen a lot of reviews here that talk about getting disconnected a bunch, but that hasn't ever happened to me (yet) so I'm not going to comment on that.

The enemies are fun to fight, but its incredibly hard to beat without teammates, as you will get overwhelmed very quickly. The enemies themselves though, have good hitboxes, aren't too strong, but aren't too weak either. A head shot will one tap, and body shots (depending on the gun) are a two shot kill. (Revolver is my favorite weapon because it one shot's anywhere)

Overall, pretty good game. Could be better, could be worse, but it has charm, and a decent storyline, with pretty good gameplay.

I'd give it a solid 7/10

—melee weapons

able to shove snow breeders

graphics

First thing to mention is that this is probably the best MULTIPLAYER zombie game out on the market. From setting your own party to getting into a zombie killing game, it is just so simple-- totally maximising the time of blasting snowy Fred's to gorey oblivion. So Vertigo, my comrades and I who enjoy blasting through levels, we thank you for the entertainment that you have given us.

HOWEVER, please don't leave this game behind to die in order to pursue others like Arizona Sunshine and all its remakes and sequels. It should totally be the other way around :-) ! I have played those other games, and while they are polished, they do not give that thrill or sh*t your pants moment when Zs come out from crooks and crannies on the side of you or up your a$$. Additionally, the ergonomics of the weapons and their attachments you can have in this game somehow feel superiorly more natural.

While, I can go yabbling on about more ways to not give up on the game, I'll instead give some suggestions on how to keep players hooked on this game and to keep the community spirit up (with what it feels is to be in a decline at the moment cos of the lack of updates :( ).

1. MAPS: please add more maps, whether you do a similar thing to Walkabout Mini Golf wherein a user must pay a couple of quid to access it and to help encourage the devs, I'm sure the community would be more than happy with it. As long as you don't along the same path as EA, it should be all well and good.
2. Boss Zombie Variations: While the ones at the moment are awesome, maybe some new ones can really change the game... including ones that can try and snatch you toward the enemy, or some sort of snow breather that adds more ice armour to zombie, slows the player down, and heals the snowbreed, or to even add a terrain change that makes it especially slippy.
3. Add some more wrist variations, like a shield, where instead of an ammo count, it could have its own lifespan that heals whenever you acquire a tape
4. Balancing weapons, when you get to horror mode, you realise the only weapons that are really viable are the hunting rifle and the auto-shotgun, the carbine, mt-47, uzis, etc get left in the dust. Perhaps add some unique quirk to each weapon to give them that leg up.
5. Weapon Stats, in the armoury, please show some sort of information regarding how much power, recoil, damage per hit, penetration strength etc- makes comparing the use of weapons far easier
6. I'm sure you've heard plenty of times, but some more weapons could be interesting, I'll leave the creativity up to you, but things like having a minigun that takes up both side pockets, or a flintlock for going down a pirate like theme etc could be interesting.
7. Achievements, those that say to complete things in modes Nightmare and Master, make it so if you choose a higher difficulty that it still counts, otherwise it's a bit dull to have to dumb down a couple of games to unlock them.

These are only some suggestions, but I would like to thank again for the entertainment you have provided, hearing any sort of response regarding this would be epic. And, for those thinking of getting the game, JUST DO IT, it's flippin fantastic-- gives you months of entertainment in itself.

​I wanted to love this game, and I truly wanted to give it a 5-star rating. Unfortunately, it just doesn’t hold up anymore.

​While the core game is decent, it feels like a relic of 2020. In 2026, the VR landscape has evolved, and there are simply far better options available. 

As many other players have suggested, the developers desperately need to give this title a full makeover. 

Upgrading the graphics and modernizing the mechanics are essential to bringing it into the current generation and making it the standout title it has the potential to be.

​The inclusion of both solo and multiplayer modes is a massive plus, but the dated aspects ultimately drag the entire package down. For now, it’s a "give it a miss."

reviews.review_title: Basically left 4 dead in vr and its good
reviews.review_description:
Like the title says, basically its left for dead in vr, its one that never leaves my library and is one of those go to game for me and the boys when we want some co op zombie shooting fun...since the game seems to no longer be receiving any more content updates, which is understandable, the only other thing i could ask for is a sequel which would be amazing..

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.
Needs updates and patching for glitches and freezing and kicking everybody out.  Fun zombie shooter but needs tweaking

I think this game is great, and I think it could be amazing if it got some updates or a remake like Arizona Sunshine did. I enjoy all projects by Vertigo Games, they are all really good, this one however, is a little complicated.

Its a great game dont get me wrong, great fun with friends, but it is a little janky, annoying and confusing at some points. I havent run into any crashes and such like the other reviews have stated but so far I do think this game is good.

If Vertigo Games made a remake of this or updated this game with better mechanics, better online networking and better graphics (graphics aren't really that bad but they could use an upgrade), I would 100% buy it again.

Must say though, if you plan on buying this game, you should wait for it to go on sale like I did, game is probably not worth $30, maybe $15 or somewhere around there (I got it for $9 🙂)

I got AS remake and thoroughly enjoyed it - so I got AS2 and loved that as well (miss you, Buddy!).  So I was looking forward to checking out this game as a spiritual successor to AS, and I am so disappointed.  
Reloading is similar but worse than AS, for one.  The concept of an 80s video arcade setup is interesting but just unnecessary and weird, for another.  Those giant "snowbreed" monsters... impossible to gauge damage inflicted, if any at all.  
This does not deserve the average star rating it has.  
There are far better games to go play.

reviews.review_title: Solid but with single player issues
reviews.review_description:
The game is pretty good though you wont get the best experience if you play alone. Yea you can get bots to help you but they literally do the bare minimum. They have infinite ammo which is a plus but all they do is shoot zombies in sight.
They dont carry consumables like grenades or heals, you cant interact with them, they will not shoot sprawl (unless its sprawl from a mission like the relay tower) (sprawl being vines that block your path and you have to shoot the yellow bits to remove), when in the final bit of a level where you have to defend a position. Generally you would want everyone to stick together. However, every so often jimmy or one of the other bots will randomly run to the other side of the area and die shortly after.
So obtaining currency and attachments has been quite a pain.

Yes im aware you can search for lobbies in the main arcade area but most lobbies i can find are stuff like the relay tower on the hardest difficulty or the first map on the easiest difficulty. Otherwise theres barely any servers even with cross play. 
And yes im aware about the discord and how you can connect with people to find lobbies and matches but im not that invested in the game to really join it.

Aside from single player stuff
There should be some easier way to obtain non duplicate attachments because ive been getting a lot of duplicates when finishing a run. And i dont even have that many attachments.

Also little nitpick but character voices (especially reloading lines) get extremely annoying

And some cool qol aspects i appreciate
Can toggle settings that can allow handswapping when dual wielding,
Toggle grab,
Weapons dont return to your belt when grabbing a consumable from your inventory
Theres also quite a bit more nice things in the settings
